meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Finish Your SaaS Build
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Learn how to use project management with Antigravity and Claude Code to overcome the founder's trap of unfinished projects.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of technical scope control. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution, you break massive, overwhelming features into atomic, shippable units. This system forces completion by aligning your product roadmap directly with your codebase, ensuring every commit drives revenue.
content.intro:
You have a graveyard of half-finished repositories. It starts with a lightning-strike idea, a surge of coffee-fueled coding, and then the inevitable 'feature creep' cliff where momentum dies. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a production-grade system to manage your cognitive load.

Real founders don't finish projects by working harder; they finish by architecting for velocity. By combining the structural discipline of Antigravity with the autonomous execution power of Claude Code, you transform your vague product vision into a rigid, manageable roadmap. It is time to stop being a hobbyist coder and start operating as a serial SaaS architect.

steps.title: Define Atomic MVP Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, isolate your MVP to one core revenue-generating feature. Use Antigravity to generate the technical requirements document.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a technical requirements document for a lean MVP focused on [Insert Core Feature]. Define the database schema, API endpoints, and UI components needed for a 48-hour build.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a high-level project roadmap based on the Antigravity TRD.'

steps.title: Bootstrap Architecture
steps.description: Generate the boilerplate and file structure. Don't waste time on configuration; automate the foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a Next.js 14 application with Supabase integration and Tailwind CSS styling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure based on the Antigravity architectural blueprint.'

steps.title: Task Decomposition
steps.description: Break the project into 2-hour work blocks. Each block must result in a deployable unit of value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose the project into sequential, atomic tasks. Order them by business value and dependency risk.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a task tracking file (tasks.md) and prioritize the first three critical path items.'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Execute the roadmap one task at a time. Use Claude Code to handle the heavy lifting of boilerplate code generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the implementation logic for the [Specific Task] component, ensuring it adheres to the existing architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the user authentication flow following the security best practices defined in the TRD.'

steps.title: Validation and Deployment
steps.description: Automate the verification of your code against the business requirements to ensure you are ready for launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for production readiness, including performance benchmarks and security audits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run test suite, verify dependencies, and prepare deployment script for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Never build more than 4 hours of code without a commit that compiles and passes a sanity check.
Use Claude Code as a pair programmer to explain why a specific refactor is necessary before applying it.
If a feature takes more than 3 days to build, it is not an MVP feature; kill it or move it to the backlog.
Treat your project management file like a live production document, not a static list.
Always validate the 'happy path' of your user journey before adding any styling or edge-case handling.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, immediately set up a feedback loop with 5 potential users. Do not add new features until you have your first dollar of revenue or a signed letter of intent.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private repo. By mastering this workflow, you move from 'aspiring builder' to 'shipping founder' in record time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
